mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0% signals labor market slack that reduces wage inflation pressures and prompts accommodative policy expectations, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative or flat in > 65% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with falling federal funds rates indicate strong consumer demand supported by cheap credit, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS declines, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning and earnings downgrade fears, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during economic slowdown regimes.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5-day returns during slowdown periods, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth expands aggregate corporate revenue pools and improves profit margins, systematically generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 growth rates across a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL monthly growth surpasses 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts reflects synchronized economic strength across labor and construction sectors, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment and reduced consumer wealth effects, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month during late-cycle downturns.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle periods, with positive return frequency exceeding 50%.
